description: External survey of Jepsen 2024-2026 analyses, Redis-Raft status, Antithesis validation, DistFuzz, and minimal reproduction research
expect: External data advances F95 B14 verification: 3-node setups validated by industry, new bug classes identified, Redis-Raft stable as reproduction target

catastrophic_findings.NATS: 49.7% acknowledged writes lost (679153/1367069) due to deferred fsync
catastrophic_findings.Bufstream: loss of acknowledged writes in healthy clusters + Kafka protocol-level bugs
catastrophic_findings.Amazon_RDS: Multi-AZ violates Snapshot Isolation via Long Fork anomalies

new_bug_classes: client-library bugs (jetcd): server correct but client introduces anomalies via incorrect retry
protocol-design bugs (Kafka transactions KAFKA-17754): affects every implementation

redis_raft_status.implication: bugs #14/#17/#19 remain stable reproduction targets (unfixed, project dormant)

antithesis_validation.funding: $105M Series A led by Jane Street (Dec 2025)
antithesis_validation.etcd_testing: 830 wall-clock hours simulating 4.5 years on 3-node + 1-node clusters
antithesis_validation.implication: DIRECTLY validates B14: 3-node clusters sufficient for finding real bugs via deterministic simulation

minimal_reproduction_research.DEMi_NSDI_2016: minimizes faulty executions by up to 97% event reduction; 9-node Raft shrunk to 5-node
minimal_reproduction_research.TaxDC_ASPLOS_2016: 104 DC bugs from 4 systems; 75% covered by ≤3 protocol initiations
minimal_reproduction_research.DistFuzz_NDSS_2025: first feedback-guided blackbox fuzzing for distributed systems; 28 confirmed bugs (20 new), 4 CVEs, no instrumentation needed

etcd_v3_6_0.described_as: potentially most reliable release in etcd history
etcd_v3_6_0.antithesis_integration: dedicated robustness tests inspired by Jepsen, integrated into CI
etcd_v3_6_0.jetcd_client_bug: official Java client still unfixed (incorrect retries, reported 2022)

cockroachdb_status.antithesis_usage: published about it but no detailed bug findings shared

diff: Expected external data to validate 3-node approach — confirmed strongly by Antithesis ($105M, 830h testing on 3-node etcd). Did NOT predict new bug classes: client-library (jetcd) and protocol-design (Kafka) are NOVEL categories not in swarm's determinism gradient. NATS 49.7% data loss is most severe Jepsen finding ever. DistFuzz as blackbox tool is directly applicable to swarm's approach. Redis-Raft dormancy confirmed — stable target.
